perturbation.original_text: 2) Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx exclusively with the Product worldwide. The aforesaid exclusivity is limited to [***] (collectively, the "Field"). Cremer may supply the Product to other customers outside of the Field.
perturbation.changed_text: 2) Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx exclusively with the Product worldwide.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Field' and the clause allowing Cremer to supply outside the Field, the contract now implies an unlimited, worldwide exclusivity. This contradicts Article 1(3), which states that Ultragenyx shall purchase the product exclusively from Cremer, implying a defined scope of exclusivity, since an exclusivity must be in a specified range to have effect, if not it cannot be legally enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3) Ultragenyx shall purchase the Product exclusively from Cremer. [***] Certain information in this document has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Confidential treatment has been requested with respect to the omitted portions. 1
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Cremer has exclusive supply rights for Ultragenyx within a defined 'Field,' allowing Cremer to supply others outside that field. The changed text removes the definition of 'Field' and the exception for Cremer to supply to others, creating a potentially unlimited exclusive supply obligation to Ultragenyx worldwide. This contradicts Article 1(3) (Contradicted Text), which states Ultragenyx shall purchase exclusively from Cremer, because the changed text broadens the scope of exclusivity which then makes it legally ambiguous. Removing the 'Field' and the ability to supply to others creates an unlimited exclusive relationship with Ultragenyx which then creates a contradiction since an exclusivity must be in a specified range to have effect, if not it cannot be legally enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The Product shall be free from defects if it is within the specifications according to Annex A.
perturbation.changed_text: The Product shall be free from defects.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Annex A, which contains the Product Specifications, the contract no longer provides a clear definition of what constitutes a 'defect'. This creates ambiguity and contradicts the intent of Article 4(5), where Ultragenyx may reject the Product if it fails to conform to the Product Specifications, which now are not clearly defined. If they are not defined, there is no base point from which the Product can be rejected, and the intent to do the specifications is unfulfilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5) Ultragenyx will perform final release of the Product. Ultragenyx may rely on the documentation provided by Cremer and Ultragenyx will not need to independently test the Product unless Ultragenyx determines such independent testing is necessary. In the event that the Product fails to conform to the Product Specifications, and/or GMP, Ultragenyx may reject the Product by giving written notice to Cremer within [***] days after receipt of the Product and all documentation (except such [***] day period will not apply for any latent defect). Within [***] days following receipt of the rejected and returned Product from Ultragenyx, Cremer will, at Ultragenyx's choice, replace such quantity of Product with Product conforming to the Product Specifications, and GMP or refund Ultragenyx the Price paid for such Product.
perturbation.justification: The modification removes the reference to Annex A, which defines the Product Specifications. Article 4(5), the 'Contradicted Text', states that Ultragenyx may reject the Product if it fails to conform to the Product Specifications. By removing the definition of Product Specifications, the basis for rejecting the product is removed, thus creating a contradiction with the right to reject based on Product Specifications as outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: Article 7 Product Development At the request and expense of Ultragenyx, Cremer shall perform development work for Ultragenyx to develop new formulations of the Product. All such work shall be performed pursuant to a statement of work (including a budget) to be agreed upon by the Parties and attached as an annex to this Agreement (each, a "Statement of Work").
perturbation.changed_text: Article 7 Product Development At the request and expense of Ultragenyx, Cremer shall perform development work for Ultragenyx to develop new formulations of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying that development work be performed pursuant to a 'Statement of Work' (including a budget) attached as an annex, the contract now lacks a defined process and budgetary control for development work. This contradicts the functionality of Article 9(3), since it mentions that 'The Annex attached to this Agreement form an integral part of the Agreement', making annexes a fundamental requirement that is not being fufilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3) The Annex attached to this Agreement form an integral part of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that development work be performed pursuant to a 'Statement of Work' attached as an annex. The changed text removes this requirement. Article 9(3) states, 'The Annex attached to this Agreement form an integral part of the Agreement.' By removing the reference to the Statement of Work annex in Article 7, the changed text contradicts Article 9(3) because an annex is now required but not necessarily present based on Article 7, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty.
